titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gary Franks Papers
titlestmt.author: Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 17651219.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 23, 2025 at 23:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection documents the professional and personal life of Congressman Gary Franks. It includes diaries, notebooks, correspondence, reports, press materials, research materials, and writings largely focused on his political career, particularly welfare reform and elections. Also present are a small quantity of papers related to his consultant career, personal papers, and 1 DVD.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Notebooks and diaries, circa 1992-2022. II. Professional papers, 1990-2017. III. Writings, circa 1996-2002. IV. Personal papers, circa 1993-2018.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.

Boxes 1-11, 17 (notebooks and diaries, election consultant correspondence, consultant career): Restricted until 2048 or five years after the death of Gary Franks, whichever occurs first. For further information consult Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.
Box 19 (DVD): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Gary Franks papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gary Franks Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Yale Archival Processing Manual.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Most folder titles were devised by the archivist at the time of processing. Original folder titles have been enclosed in quotation marks. Additions to original folder titles have been enclosed in brackets.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gary Franks (1953- ) is an American politician who represented Connecticut's 5th district in the United States House of Representatives from 1991 to 1997. Franks was the first African American elected to Congress from Connecticut and the first modern Black conservative elected to the House of Representatives. He was born in Waterbury, Connecticut and received a B.A. in sociology from Yale University in 1975. After an unsuccessful Senate run in 1998, Franks retired from politics. He became a consultant, founding the firms Franks and Genua Associates and Gary Alvin Associates. He has also taught at Georgetown University, Hampton University, and the University of Virginia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the professional and personal life of Congressman Gary Franks. It includes diaries, notebooks, correspondence, reports, press materials, research materials, and writings largely focused on his political career, particularly welfare reform and elections. A significant portion of the professional papers stem from Franks's work as chair of the Welfare Reform Task Force. Records relating to elections include press releases, correspondence, and voter surveys. Also present are a small quantity of papers related to his consultant career, personal papers, and 1 DVD.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received to maintain Franks's original filing system and connections between dated and undated notebooks in each folder and adjacent folders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks and diaries documenting the life and career of Congressman Gary Franks, including his political career, work as a consultant, family life, and religious reflections.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until 2048 or five years after the death of Gary Franks, whichever occurs first.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Congressional career, 1990-1999. Consultant career, 1997-2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports, research materials, contracts, meeting minutes, and press releases documenting Franks's career as a Congressman and consultant, including elections, welfare reform, and correspondence with conservative think tanks.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five groupings: Elections, Welfare reform, Urban Entrepreneurial Opportunities Act, Press materials, and Correspondence.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until 2048 or five years after the death of Gary Franks, whichever occurs first.

did.unittitle: Congressional Research Services reports on welfare reform
Welfare reform correspondence, meeting notes, and research materials
Urban Entrepreneurial Opportunities Act correspondence, drafts, and research materials

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Outgoing correspondence is filed first in chronological order, followed by incoming correspondence in chronological order.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until 2048 or five years after the death of Gary Franks, whichever occurs first.

did.unittitle: Searching for the Promised Land excerpts
Eggs in One Basket outline and notes
